name: Muqsit ibn Zuhayr
title: Third Taghlibi Brother
shortDescription: The other brother of Qasit and Kurdus who left Kufa with them in the dark of night and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Muqsit ibn Zuhayr al-Taghlibi was the third brother of the brave Taghlibi family. Like his brothers Qasit and Kurdus, he was a pure Shia of Kufa and an old companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS).

When Ibn Ziyad turned Kufa into a military garrison so no one could join Imam Hussain (AS), Muqsit, along with his brothers, mounted their horses and reached the Imam's caravan in Karbala through desert routes in the dead of night.

Muqsit stood beside the Imam throughout the difficult moments before Ashura. When the morning of Ashura arrived and Umar Sa'd's brutal all-out attack (known as the first attack) began, Muqsit drew his sword along with his brothers.

He fought bravely shoulder to shoulder with his brothers and, after repelling a large part of the enemy forces, was martyred in that very first attack, entering Paradise with his brothers.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى مُقْسِطِ بْنِ زُهَيْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Rajabiyya:) Peace be upon Muqsit ibn Zuhayr al-Taghlibi.
